title: Magnetoencephalographic (MEG) Pitch and Duration Mismatch Negativity (MMN) in First-Episode Psychosis
description: This magnetoencephalographic (MEG) dataset comprises auditory oddball paradigm recordings from participants with first-episode psychosis and healthy controls, designed to investigate mismatch negativity (MMN) responses to pitch and duration deviants. The study includes three participant groups: healthy controls, first-episode schizophrenia, and first-episode affective psychosis patients. Data were collected as part of the Pittsburgh Early Psychosis Program between December 2017 and September 2021. The dataset includes clinical, neuropsychological, demographic, and medication data in the derivatives.
methods_description: MEG recordings were acquired during an auditory oddball paradigm featuring standard tones, pitch deviants, and duration deviants. Participant groups included healthy controls (HC), first-episode schizophrenia (FESZ), and first-episode affective psychosis (FEAFF) patients. Clinical, neuropsychological, demographic, and medication data are provided in the derivatives.
